content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Linda Bunch 
Registrar of Voters 
County of San Diego 
P.o. Box 85093 
San Diego, CA 
92138-5093 
Dear Ms. Bunch: 
June 14, 1989 
Re: Combined Campaign Report 
Our File No. 1-89-355 
This is to confirm advice provided to you on June 7, 1989, 
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the political 
Reform Act (the "Act").1./ 
This letter constitutes written advice 
under section 84205. 
section 84205 states: 
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit 
candidates and committees to file campaign statements combin-
ing statements and reports required to be filed by this 
title. 
A special election has been called for Assembly District 76. 
The office became vacant on June 1, 1989. 
The primary election 
date is August 8, 1989, and a run-off election is to be held on 
October 3, 1989. 
Because the first pre-election statement due in 
connection with the August 8, 1989 election (Section 84200.8) and 
the semi-annual statement due for the first six months of 1989 
(Section 84200) cover almost identical periods, we believe it is 
appropriate for candidates being voted on in that election, their 
1./ Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 
18000, et ~ 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Linda Bunch 
Page Two 
controlled committees and committees primarily formed to support 
or oppose candidates in that election to combine those two 
reports. 
Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on 
the following schedule: 
~ 
of statement 
Period Covered 
Filing Deadline 
1st Pre-Election (for 
Jan. 1 - June 24 
June 29 
Aug. election) 
combined with Semi-Annual 
2nd Pre-Election 
June 25 - July 22 
July 27 
for Aug. election 
Pre-run-off Electionl/ 
July 23 - Sept. 16 
September 21 
for Oct. Election 
Semi-Annual 
Sept. 171/ -
Dec. 31 
January 31, 1990 
The contribution limitations imposed by Proposition 73 apply 
separately for a special election cycle and for a special runoff 
election cycle. 
The limitations for each of these cycles are the 
same amounts as the limitations for a fiscal year. 
Contributors 
may contribute up to the stated limits (see attached) in the 
special election cycle even if they already contributed the 
maximum amount during the fiscal year. 
In addition, contributors 
may contribute up to the stated limits in the special runoff 
election cycle even if they contributed the maximum amounts in the 
fiscal year and in the special election cycle. 
l/ Candidates who lose the August 8, 1989 election and their 
controlled committees are not required to file the pre-run-off 
election statement due on September 21, 1989. 
1J Semi-annual statements filed by candidates and committees not 
involved in the run-off election will cover July 23 -
December 31, 
1989. 


Linda Bunch 
Page Three 
"Special election cycle" means the day on which the office 
becomes vacant until the day of the special election. 
"Special runoff election cycle" means the day after the 
special election until the day of the special runoff election. 
If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not 
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662. 
cc: 
Secretary of State 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
By: 
Sandra Taylor 
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: How should candidates and committees in a special election (Assembly District 76, with primary on August 8, 1989 and runoff on October 3, 1989) file their campaign disclosure statements when multiple reporting periods overlap, and what contribution limitations apply under Proposition 73 for special election and runoff cycles?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that candidates, controlled committees, and committees supporting/opposing candidates in the special election may combine their first pre-election statement (covering January 1 - June 24) with the semi-annual statement due for the first six months of 1989, with filing on June 29. Contribution limitations under Proposition 73 apply separately for the special election cycle and special runoff cycle, with the same amounts as fiscal year limits, and contributors may contribute up to stated limits in each cycle independently.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains clear advisory content formatted as a letter. The question and conclusion are reconstructed from the letter body and advice given. A contribution limits table is included as supporting material.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should candidates and committees in a special election (Assembly District 76, with primary on August 8, 1989 and runoff on October 3, 1989) file their campaign disclosure statements when multiple reporting periods overlap, and what contribution limitations apply under Proposition 73 for special election and runoff cycles?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that candidates, controlled committees, and committees supporting/opposing candidates in the special election may combine their first pre-election statement (covering January 1 - June 24) with the semi-annual statement due for the first six months of 1989, with filing on June 29. Contribution limitations under Proposition 73 apply separately for the special election cycle and special runoff cycle, with the same amounts as fiscal year limits, and contributors may contribute up to stated limits in each cycle independently.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming oral advice regarding campaign disclosure filing schedules for a special Assembly District 76 election with combined reporting periods and clarifying that Proposition 73 contribution limitations apply separately to the special election cycle and special runoff election cycle.
